message:
feat(fuse): harden MOVE_EXT donor fd and source eligibility (bd-4d3xf)

Close out bd-4d3xf (harden ext4 EXT4_IOC_MOVE_EXT donor validation
parity) by tightening the FUSE EXT4_IOC_MOVE_EXT decode path so
invalid donor descriptors and ineligible source inodes are rejected
up front with the same errnos the kernel ext4 driver uses, rather
than silently reinterpreted or carried further into the move engine.

crates/ffs-fuse/src/lib.rs:
- Decode the donor fd field as i32 (the native kernel type) before
  coercing to u32. A negative donor_fd now short-circuits to EBADF
  so pathological / sentinel values are rejected before we even
  consult the fd table — matching the kernel's ERRNO for "bad file
  descriptor" instead of EINVAL.
- The u32 conversion is done via `try_from` with an EBADF fallback
  so any residual negative case is contained at the boundary.
- Introduce validate_move_ext_source(attr, flags) that gates the
  source inode on three kernel-documented preconditions:
    * EINVAL if the source is not a regular file,
    * EINVAL if the source is zero-length (there is nothing to move),
    * EOPNOTSUPP if the source does not have EXT4_EXTENTS_FL set,
      because MOVE_EXT is only supported on extent-based inodes.
  Import EXT4_EXTENTS_FL from ffs_types to drive the flag check.
- Adjust parse_move_ext_args to return the Ok tuple via the new u32
  fallibility so EBADF propagates from parse instead of panicking on
  a misvalued donor_fd.

.beads/issues.jsonl:
- bd-4d3xf close_reason: "Hardened MOVE_EXT ioctl donor-fd and source
  eligibility validation" aligned with the new errno contract.

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
